meaning: adj.放出空气的,通货紧缩的,物价低廉(尤指成本不降低时的反常情形)的
examples: This was a highly deflationary cocktail.
DE Roquefort's voice had a calculating and deflationary effect.
Unemployment remains stubbornly high while measures of inflation are getting awfully close to deflationary territory.
There is only the prospect of Americans suffering through either a deflationary depression or hyperinflation.
